note: gilānāgilānānaṃ = gilāna+agilānānaṃ (sick and not-sick); sandhi false friend
polysemy=true; metalinguistic gloss 'nāgasaddassa ahināga-hatthināgesu pavattanato' (snake/elephant senses) applied as buddhanāga = the Buddha as nāga (na+āgu honorific)
mahānāgassā glosses mahāvarāha rejecting boar-sense; great beast tearing earth (padālentassa, mahiṃ vikubbato) — window cannot fix serpent vs elephant
nāgassa danto = elephant's tusk -> nāgadantaka (ivory peg); aṅkusa = goad, gajamatthaka

claim: A monk overpowers the nāga's fiery majesty (teja/ānubhāva) by his own power
note: surāpāna sikkhāpada gloss; 'attano tejasā nāgassa teja'nti' = overcoming a nāga's teja (the Sāgata nāga-subduing episode)
nāggisantatta = na+aggi+santatta (not heated by fire); sandhi false friend
gilānāgilānassa = gilāna+agilānassa (sick and not-sick); sandhi false friend
gilānāgilānānaṃ = gilāna+agilānānaṃ (sick and not-sick); sandhi false friend
gilānāgilānassa = gilāna+agilānassa (sick and not-sick); sandhi false friend
sāmikānāgamaṃ = sāmikānaṃ anāgamaṃ (non-coming of owners); glossed 'sāmikānaṃ anāgamanaṃ'; an-āgama prefix false friend
gamanāgame = gamana+āgame (going and coming); sandhi false friend
sāmikānāgamaṃ = sāmikānaṃ anāgamaṃ (non-coming of owners); an-āgama prefix false friend
nānāgabbhā = nānā+gabbhā (various inner rooms/cabins); sandhi false friend
